electron_main_process.framework: Electron 41 + Effect-TS (Effect.gen for orchestration, Effect.runFork to launch)
electron_main_process.role: App lifecycle root — logging, crash reporter, proxy config, sidecar spawn, window creation, IPC handler registration, deep links, menus, updater

sidecar_utility_process.role: Forked via Electron utilityProcess.fork(). Dynamically imports virtual:opencode-server, runs optional SQLite JsonMigration, starts Effect-based HTTP server

preload_bridge.security: contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, sandbox: false

renderer_solidjs_ui.role: UI entry — creates Platform provider with desktop-native APIs, wraps @opencode-ai/app AppInterface

loading_screen.role: Separate BrowserWindow shown during sidecar init and migrations. Shows progress via IPC push events INIT_STEP and SQLITE_MIGRATION_PROGRESS

shared_ui_package.role: Core app shell with routing, providers, error boundaries, connection gating

effect_service_runtime.role: Merges ~54 service layers (Npm, Bus, Auth, Config, Git, File, FileWatcher, Plugin, Provider, Agent, MCP, ToolRegistry, etc.) into a single AppLayer, creates ManagedRuntime

http_server.role: Effect-based HTTP server (@effect/platform + HttpRouter), started by sidecar with port fallback

phase_1_electron_main.description: Electron main process startup — packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts (all inside Effect.gen, forked via Effect.runFork)

steps.detail: Right-click context menu config (save image, disable search options)
CD to home dir for ripgrep compatibility. Silently caught on failure.
Disables embedded web UI for desktop
If OPENCODE_TEST_ONBOARDING=1: creates temp dir, sets :memory: DB, XDG dirs
App identity and data directory setup
Electron-log initialization with per-scope files, console transport, log rotation (7 day cleanup)
Crashpad startup (local only, no upload). Crash dumps in userData/Crashpad/

steps.action: Set default CA certificates (system + default merge)
steps.detail: Non-critical — caught and logged on failure, continues
Ensures loopback/localhost bypass NO_PROXY, enables global proxy from env
proxy-bypass-list, enable-features (js call stacks in crash reports), remote-debugging-port=9222 (dev only)
If another instance: quit immediately
Loads shell env and sets OPENCODE_CLIENT=desktop, file watcher/icon discovery flags
second-instance (deep links), open-url, before-quit/will-quit (kill sidecar), child-process-gone, render-process-gone, SIGINT/SIGTERM
Kill sidecar → app.relaunch() → app.exit(0)
All IPC handlers (init, config, store, fs, session, window, locale, github auth, plugin transport, capabilities)
Electron app ready
Tauri data migration (best-effort, skipped if already migrated, non-blocking)
Deep link protocol registration
Custom oc:// protocol for serving renderer files
macOS dock icon
electron-updater config (autoUpdate if packaged and not dev)
Chrome net log capture (non-critical, caught)
Check if opencode.db exists in XDG_DATA_HOME. False for :memory: mode.
Bind ephemeral port (0 -> random port via net.createServer) or use OPENCODE_PORT env
fork utilityProcess with sidecar.js, send start command, poll health check every 100ms (30s timeout)
If migration needed and loading takes >1s: show loading window
Wait for sidecar to be healthy
Notify renderer that init is complete
Open main BrowserWindow (1280x800, sandboxed preload, custom titlebar)
macOS native menu (trigger, checkForUpdates, relaunch)
Close loading overlay when main window ready
Set OPENCODE_SERVER_USERNAME, OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD, XDG_STATE_HOME
All caught and logged on failure, non-blocking
Imports { Database, JsonMigration, Log, Server } from compiled server bundle
Sidecar-side logging init
If needsMigration=true: runs SQLite JSON migrations, sends progress updates via parentPort
Starts Effect-based HTTP server on the negotiated port. After ready: postMessage({type:'ready'})
On any error: postMessage({type:'error'}) then process.exit(1)
Fetches updaterEnabled flag. Caught: defaults to {updaterEnabled: false}
Fallback: document.hasFocus()
Retrieves any pending deep links from main process
Listens for init step events, returns server URL/password when ready
Loads locale preference, falls back to legacy storage
Caught: defaults to null
Caught: defaults to disabled
Caught: defaults to undefined
<Show when={!defaultServer.loading && !sidecar.loading && !windowConfig.loading && !windowCount.loading && !locale.loading}> — UI hidden until all resources load
SolidJS component tree mount — theme provider, i18n, router, error boundaries, server sync

phase_4_effect_runtime.description: Effect service runtime — packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts (lazy, on first demand)

steps.detail: Layer.mergeAll(54 services) — all lazy, not initialized yet
Runtime creation — still lazy, services built on first yield*
Eager init: Config.get() → Plugin.init() → parallel init of Reference, LSP, ShareNext, Format, File, FileWatcher, Vcs, Snapshot, Project
Forked background fiber: runs session_usage_from_messages migration against Database

items.pattern: ConfigService.Service (env vars OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL/SSL/POOL_SIZE)
Effect service — if url set → PGlite/Pool+Drizzle, else → SQLite adapter
Non-Effect singleton. Lazy init on first call. Resolves path from RuntimeFlags
Effect service — forks background fiber for migration run
NOT initialized at startup. Registered lazily per-session in acp-next/service.ts:915 via registerMcpServers()
Effect service — OAuth token management per server URL
Eager init in bootstrap.ts:37. Loads 9 built-in auth plugins + external plugins from config. 'Plugin can mutate config so it has to be initialized before anything else'
Effect service — streamText from ai SDK with middleware
Effect service — sub-agent capability using streamObject
Effect service — orchestrates LLM call → tool execution → result handling
Effect service — CRUD for sessions, stored in SQLite/PG via Database
Effect service — registers ~60 built-in tools (Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Bash, Task, etc.) + plugin tools in parallel
Effect service — loads config files + env vars
ConfigService.Service — ~30 OPENCODE_* env vars as feature flags
PG connection string, SSL, pool size from env
Server authentication config
Layer.provideMerge — OpenTelemetry tracing setup
From @effect/opentelemetry/Tracer, used for ai SDK experimental_telemetry
Effect service — @parcel/watcher native bindings, lazy-load backend
Effect service — read/write/search with Ripgrep
Effect service — project DB CRUD, VCS root detection
Effect service — git operations, subscribes to FileWatcher events

security.exposed_api: window.api with 67 typed methods (no raw ipcRenderer access)
security.validated: ipc-validation.ts validates agent/MCP/plugin configs before storage
security.hostname_restricted: GitHub API proxy restricted to api.github.com, uploads.github.com

init_pattern.renderer_calls: window.api.awaitInitialization(onStep) → ipcRenderer.on('init-step', onStep) + typedInvoke('await-initialization') → main sends streaming events via webContents.send + one-shot ServerReadyData via ipcMain.handle

critical_failures_block_startup.failure: Sidecar spawn failure (60s timeout / error message)
critical_failures_block_startup.behavior: App init rejects permanently. No retry mechanism.
Process dies — NO global uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handler

non_critical_failures_continue.behavior: Logged error, app continues (server may be slow)

recovery_patterns.name: wireWindowRecovery
recovery_patterns.dialog: Modal dialog with: Relaunch / Export Logs / Quit / Keep Waiting (unresponsive only)

recovery_patterns.name: unresponsive sampler
recovery_patterns.detail: Collects JS stacks every 1s for 15s, logs on flush. No auto-recovery.

recovery_patterns.name: React AppErrorBoundary (per-route)
recovery_patterns.detail: Each route has its own boundary — one crash doesn't break others

recovery_patterns.name: ConnectionGate retry
recovery_patterns.detail: Server health check polling every 1s, 10s blocking phase → background phase

recovery_patterns.name: console transport degradation
recovery_patterns.detail: If stdout is broken pipe (EPIPE), console logging disabled silently

gaps: No global uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers — any unexpected main process exception crashes without recovery
Sidecar has no retry/backoff — single 60s timeout then permanent failure
No degraded mode / safe mode — it's full startup or failure with recovery dialog
Sentry GlobalHandlers filtered out in production — prod crashes only caught by explicit ErrorBoundary captureException calls
No exponential backoff in health check polling

relevant.summary: applyMigrations defined at db.pg.ts:25 but never called anywhere in runtime
migration-pg/ has 1 migration vs migration/ has 20 — massive gap

architectural_observations: Three distinct process layers: Electron main (Node.js), sidecar utility process (Node.js), and renderer (SolidJS sandbox). Each has separate start sequence.

The Electron main process is surprisingly thin — its job is to spawn the sidecar, handle deep links/window management/OS integration, and proxy IPC. All business logic lives in the Effect runtime inside the sidecar.
Effect-TS is used throughout for structured concurrency (Effect.gen, Deferred, Fiber, Layer). The main init sequence is a single Effect.gen orchestrated with Deferred for cross-task synchronization.

The IPC bridge is extremely well-typed — channel constants in ipc-channels.ts, contracts in ipc-contract.ts, preload bridge in preload/index.ts, all using the same IPC constant. There's even a validation script (scripts/validate-ipc-migration.ts).
The sidecar spawn is the single critical path. port:0 negotiation, fork + health poll + migration is entirely sequential within the loading Task fiber. No retry mechanism.
Database adapter selection (SQLite vs PGlite/PG) happens at Effect runtime construction time based on OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL env var. The runtime doesn't restart if the DB connection fails.

The renderer init gates the entire UI behind a Show component waiting on 5 async resources (defaultServer, sidecar, windowConfig, windowCount, locale). This creates a hard block — all five must resolve (or fail with defaults) before any UI renders.

The opencode package (packages/opencode) is built separately via script/build-node.ts and consumed by the sidecar via a virtual import (virtual:opencode-server). This is a surprising build-time coupling — the server bundle must exist before the desktop app can run.

Plugin loading is the only service that has an explicit 'init before everything else' comment (bootstrap.ts:37) because plugins can mutate config. All other services initialize lazily on first demand.
The three init phases (server_waiting → sqlite_waiting → done) are communicated from main to renderer via IPC push. The loading.tsx screen can display a progress bar for SQLite migrations.
